response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of washes. Since a leap year has 366 days and she washes her hair every other day, she will wash her hair approximately 183 times. Multiplying 183 washes by the 1/4 ounce used per wash results in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces of shampoo. With each bottle containing 14 ounces, she would need to divide 45.75 by 14, which equals roughly 3.27, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to have enough supply for the entire year. Please send the order confirmation to [email address] so we can verify the shipment details.

Brittany should ensure she orders the shampoo before the current stock runs out to avoid any interruptions in her routine. The total cost will depend on the current price listed at [store location], which may vary based on seasonal promotions. By planning ahead and buying the four required bottles, she secures her supply for the full 366 days without needing an emergency trip to the store. Remember to check the expiration dates on the new bottles upon arrival to ensure freshness.
